response:
"Sec. 1  
[The given equation is a combination of a fourth-degree polynomial and an absolute value function. Because of the absolute value, the equation is not symmetric about the y-axis, but it is symmetric about the y-axis when considering both positive and negative values of the variable. This symmetry implies that if a solution exists for a positive value, there is likely a corresponding solution for the negative value.]  

Sec. 2  
[By analyzing the behavior of the function on both the positive and negative sides of the variable, it can be determined that the equation has exactly four real number solutions. This conclusion is based on the fact that the function increases without bound as the variable moves away from zero in either direction, and it crosses the horizontal line representing the constant value at two points on each side of the y-axis.]"
